bird.name: 新西兰岩鹩
bird.summary: 新西兰岩鹩（台湾作石異鷯，学名：Xenicus gilviventris），是雀形目刺鹩科的一种鸟类。 新西兰岩鹩体重约17.59克，翼长约49毫米，嘴峰长约13.8毫米，喙宽度约3.9毫米，喙厚度约3.3毫米，跗蹠长约23.2毫米，尾长约13.3毫米。新西兰岩鹩在其分布区内为…

locations.name: 南阿爾卑斯山脈
locations.summary: 南阿爾卑斯山脈（英語：Southern Alps，毛利語：Kā Tiritiri-o-te-Moana）是位於紐西蘭南島西部的一條山脈，是整個南島的天然分水嶺。「南阿爾卑斯山脈」一般是用來泛指整座山脈，組成這座山脈的其他眾多小山脈有許多各…

locations.name: Westland (New Zealand electorate)
locations.summary: Westland was a parliamentary electorate in the West Coast of New Zealand from 1866 to 1868 and 1890 to 1972.

relations.subject: 新西兰岩鹩
relations.evidence: |title=Rock wren Pīwauwau |url=http://www.nzbirdsonline.org.nz/species/rock-wren |website=New Zealand Birds Online}} of the Southern Alps, the Tasman Mountains of Northwest Nelson, and the Victoria Range of Westland, all in the South Island; it is New Zealand's only truly alpine bird. Subfossil remains suggest before Polynesian settlement it was also found in lowland forest

relations.subject: 新西兰岩鹩
relations.evidence: | year=2018| title= Control of invasive predators improves breeding success of an endangered alpine passerine | journal=Ibis| volume=160| pages= 892–899 | doi=10.1111/ibi.12617| issue=4|s2cid= 91028336 }} The long-term effect of climate change on their alpine habitat is also a threat, as warmer temperatures will allow rats to move higher into the mountains. In 2008–2010, a total of 40 rock wrens were translocated to Secretary Island, an rodent-free island in Fiordland, the third-tallest island in New Zealand. In 2010 a survey located 12 unbanded rock wrens, indicating they were successfully breeding. The rock wren was voted New Zealand Bird of the Year for 2022
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
